title: Layered Web Application
triggers: user requests go from frontend to backend to database
layered web application with separate presentation and backend layers
web client talks to an application backend

requiredNodes.label: User
requiredNodes.description: Person or external actor interacting with the application.

requiredNodes.label: Web Frontend
requiredNodes.description: Presentation layer that collects user input and renders output.

requiredNodes.label: Application Backend
requiredNodes.description: Backend layer that owns business logic and data access.

requiredNodes.label: Primary Datastore
requiredNodes.description: Persistent store used by the backend layer.

optionalNodes.label: Cache
optionalNodes.description: Optional read cache for popular application data.

optionalNodes.label: Search Index
optionalNodes.description: Optional search-specific index for fast query and discovery.

invariants.rule: The Web Frontend should call the Application Backend rather than reading or writing the Primary Datastore directly.
invariants.rationale: The backend owns business logic and persistence concerns in a layered application.

invariants.rule: The Application Backend must have a data edge to the Primary Datastore.
invariants.rationale: The backend layer persists or retrieves the application state.

invariants.rule: Optional Cache or Search Index components should be mediated by the Application Backend.
invariants.rationale: Supporting stores should not bypass the core application layer.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt emphasizes queues, asynchronous workers, or pipeline stages.
antiPatterns.rationale: Those traits suggest a pipeline or background-processing pattern rather than a simple layered app.

antiPatterns.condition: The prompt describes services exchanging events through a broker.
antiPatterns.rationale: An event-driven topology is structurally different from a layered web application.

clarifications.question: Should the frontend be modeled as a browser UI, a mobile client, or a generic presentation layer?
clarifications.reason: The presentation label can be adapted without changing the core topology.

clarifications.question: Do you want cache or search components shown, or should the diagram stay minimal?
clarifications.reason: Supporting stores are common but optional additions to the layered pattern.
